data.title: Braskem Downgraded to Sell: Weak Petrochemical Cycle, Rising Liquidity Risks, and Unsustainable Cash Burn Undermine Rally
data.description:
In a report released on March 25, Leonardo Marcondes from Bank of America Securities downgraded Braskem S.A. to a Sell, with a price target of BRL7.50.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Leonardo Marcondes has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors tied mainly to Braskem’s deteriorating financial profile and industry backdrop. He highlights that the company is likely to keep consuming cash amid weak petrochemical margins, a stronger Brazilian real, softer-than-expected demand, and operational setbacks, all of which have already produced sizeable negative cash flow and are projected to do so again in 2026.

In parallel, he stresses growing liquidity risks, noting upcoming U.S.-dollar debt maturities that tighten Braskem’s flexibility just as its fundamentals remain fragile. Marcondes also points to an extended downcycle in petrochemicals driven by large global capacity additions, with no quick recovery in spreads in sight, and reflects this tougher environment by increasing the company’s cost of capital and cutting his price objective, concluding the recent share rally is not supported by underlying earnings power.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
